channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Dozens of survivors and dead pulled from abandoned South African mine as hundreds remain underground
item.description: Using a cage-like device, South African rescuers have brought at least 60 bodies and 92 survivors to the surface from one of the country's deepest mines.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean law enforcement officials enter presidential compound to detain impeached Yoon
item.description: Seemingly hundreds of law enforcement officials have entered the residential compound of impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol in the capital Seoul in their second attempt to detain him over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Puerto Rico asks Trump for help after Venezuela's Maduro threatens invasion
item.description: Puerto Rico’s new Republican governor is drawing attention to Venezuelan strongman Nicolás Maduro’s military threats against the U.S. island in the wake of President-elect Trump’s calls for U.S. territorial expansion. In a letter to Trump dated Monday, Gov.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: What is ASEAN, union referenced in Hegseth hearing
item.description: Sen. Tammy Duckworth put Pete Hegseth’s foreign policy chops to the test during his confirmation hearing on Tuesday as President-elect Trump’s pick to lead the Pentagon.
item.dc:creator: Colin Meyn, The Hill

item.title: Biden to lift Cuba terrorism designation, reversing Trump decision in final week in office
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Tuesday that it will remove Cuba from the list of State Sponsors of Terrorism (SST) in exchange for the release of political prisoners jailed for protesting against the regime in July 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly half worldwide hold antisemitic views: ADL
item.description: Nearly half of people worldwide hold antisemitic views, according to a new survey by the Anti-Defamation League (ADL).
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: France’s new prime minister says he'll renegotiate a contested plan to raise the retirement age
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s new prime minister François Bayrou announced Tuesday the renegotiation of a contested plan raising the retirement age from 62 to 64, in a crucial move to seek more stability for his minority government.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greece hotel pools set to switch to seawater on drought-hit islands
item.description: Hotels on Greek islands may be filling their swimming pools with seawater this summer in response to worsening drought conditions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UN migration agency says gang violence in Haiti leads to tripling of internally displaced people
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The U.N. migration agency says internal displacement in Haiti, largely caused by gang violence, has tripled over the last year and now surpasses 1 million people — a record in the Caribbean nation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Zelenskyy, Macron discuss Western troop deployment in Ukraine before German defense chief visits
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said that he has held further discussions with French President Emmanuel Macron about the possibility of Western troops deploying in Ukraine to safeguard any peace deal ending the nearly three-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Irregular migration into the European Union fell sharply last year, border agency says
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The number of irregular border crossings into the European Union fell significantly in 2024, according to the bloc's border control agency Frontex, something which it attributed to intensified cooperation against smuggling networks.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia's top diplomat praises Trump's views on Ukraine conflict
item.description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov says Moscow is open for talks with President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Vladimir Isachenkov, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill 18 in Gaza, medics say, as Hamas accepts a ceasefire draft
item.description: Israeli strikes in the Gaza Strip killed at least 18 people overnight including women and children, health officials said Tuesday, as Israel and Hamas appeared to be narrowing in on a ceasefire deal to end the 15-month war and release dozens of hostages.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO announces a new mission to protect undersea cables in the Baltic Sea region
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO is launching a new mission to protect undersea cables in the Baltic Sea region after a string of incidents that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the strategic region, the alliance's leader said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken makes the case for post-war reconstruction, security and governance of Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken has made a last-minute case for a plan for the post-war reconstruction and governance of Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas appears tantalizingly close to completion.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hamas has accepted draft agreement for Gaza cease-fire, hostage release: officials
item.description: Hamas has reportedly agreed to a draft of a multi-phase cease-fire deal with Israel that would include the release of hostages who were taken into Gaza on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ugandan military court rules it can try opposition figure on treachery charge carrying death penalty
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — A prominent opposition figure in Uganda will stand trial on the serious charge of treachery, a military court ruled Tuesday, escalating the legal trouble Kizza Besigye faces ahead of presidential elections scheduled for 2026.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: European defense heavyweights say meeting Trump's military spending target won't be easy
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The defense ministers of Europe's five top military spenders said Monday they want to continue increasing their investments in defense but described meeting President-elect Donald Trump's challenge for them to raise spending to 5% of their overall economic output as complicated
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA and MONIKA SCISLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some Israeli soldiers refuse to keep fighting in Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Yotam Vilk says the image of Israeli soldiers killing an unarmed Palestinian teenager in the Gaza Strip is seared in his mind. An officer in the armored corps, Vilk said the instructions were to shoot any unauthorized person who entered an Israeli-controlled buffer zone in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Head of the International Court of Justice is named Lebanon's new prime minister
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s new president has asked prominent diplomat and jurist Nawaf Salam to form the country’s new government after Salam was named prime minister by a large number of legislators Monday. The move apparently angered the Hezbollah group and its allies.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada ready to buy more American products to appease Trump's tariff threat, ambassador says
item.description: PALM DESERT, Calif. (AP) — Canada's ambassador to Washington said Monday that Canada is prepared to buy more from the United States, including its next fleet of submarines, to appease President-elect Donald Trump's concerns about the U.S.-Canada trade deficit.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico sends firefighters to Los Angeles to help battle devastating wildfires
item.description: Disaster relief workers from the National Forestry Commission and the Ministry of Defense in Mexico also arrived to assist local emergency workers.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Significant progress made in talks over Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage release, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — U.S. and Arab mediators made significant progress overnight toward brokering a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war and the release of scores of hostages held in the Gaza Strip, but a deal hasn't been reached yet, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: VICTORIA EASTWOOD, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says he’s leaving Trump ‘strong hand to play,' defends his record on Afghanistan
item.description: Biden spoke for the last time on U.S. foreign policy and national security, and an issue seen as one of his greatest blunders, the withdrawal from Afghanistan, did not escape mentioning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian photographer Oliviero Toscani, famed for provocative Benetton campaigns, dies at 82
item.description: MILAN (AP) — Oliviero Toscani, the photographer behind Benetton’s provocative ad campaigns of the 1980s and 1990s who later broke with the Italian knitwear brand amid controversy, died Monday at age 82. Toscani disclosed last year he had a rare disease.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada deploying 60 firefighters to help with California blazes
item.description: In a post on X, Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Canada was helping aid the Los Angeles area as several wildfires continue devastating the area.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Biden admin slammed for ‘waiting’ to declare genocide in Sudan
item.description: The incoming Chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee has blasted the Biden Admnin for waiting until just 13 days before their term ends to declare atrocities in Sudan’s war as genocide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lebanon's new president strikes a nationalistic tone amid regional shifts, further weakening of Hezbollah
item.description: The Lebanese parliament finally voted on a new president on Thursday following two years of deadlock. Joseph Aoun the head of the country's armed forces won a majority of the votes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
